chapters.title: When tidying is not for everyone
chapters.text:
Tidying is not a talent that comes naturally; it’s a skill that develops with dedication and experience. Age is no barrier to being organized, but avoidable mistakes can occur without proper attention to techniques. If you want to be skilled at tidying, you need to be familiar with its methods and the potential challenges you can face.

Making room for new, precious things requires discarding the unnecessary ones.

Tidying and storage go hand in hand. While thinking about which objects you want to discard, you also think about where to store the ones you want to keep. Remember that sorting clutter, discarding unimportant things, and creating storage doesn’t mean you’ve completed or mastered the art of tidying. Simply arranging all your books doesn't mean you've tidied; you've just moved the clutter from one place to another. Remember to discard first! That way, you won’t repack the unnecessary clutter you planned to eliminate. Mastering the strategies behind tidying can enhance your life in numerous ways.

Embark on this journey to uncover detailed techniques and strategies to help you effectively clear your space. As you follow along, the significance of organizing your surroundings and the reasons behind eliminating unnecessary things will become clear. Say goodbye to adapting to your environment; instead, say hello to tailoring your surroundings to your needs, desires, and values.

chapters.title: Confronting yourself in a household setting
chapters.text:
The challenge of decluttering often arises from a blurred line between cleaning and tidying. While the terms often appear interchangeably, they are two entirely different things. Thus, the first step to maintaining a tidy home is recognizing the differences between the two.

Even though both aim to make a room sparkle, tidying involves removing objects, while cleaning means eliminating dirt. Clutter doesn't spontaneously appear; it enters when you bring or allow it in. Ultimately, the mess is your responsibility, making tidying a self-reflective process that forces you to confront yourself.

Cleaning, on the other hand, is caused by dirt. Dirt accumulates on its own, and it is normal for dust and dirt to pile up. Therefore, unlike tidying, cleaning requires you to confront nature.

While tidying requires you to keep things that bring you joy, there’s nothing to keep when you clean. So, prioritize tidying before cleaning.

The best way to identify what brings you joy is to discern how close it is to your heart.

Although it may seem challenging to determine whether an item brings you joy, comparing it with similar items often yields the most clarity. This comparative approach helps you clarify your emotions. For example, when you are unsure which shoe to keep, you can compare it with other shoes. Selecting the one you like makes it easy to discard the other.

You could say that tidying orders the mind while cleaning purifies it. ~ Marie Kondo

Tidying is not limited to deciding what to keep and what to discard. Instead, it's a great opportunity that allows you to reconsider whether this variety of things corresponds to your lifestyle and reflects your personality and values.

chapters.title: Cultivating joy through your living space
chapters.text:
When you discard things, the result is nothing but a bare space that brings you no joy. When tidying, the primary goal should be to create a space that is a haven of items that make you happy.

The first step to creating a beautiful home is to decorate it with the things you love. Although you shouldn’t hold back, you should avoid overdoing it with decorations. Too much interior decoration is also cluttering.

The next step is to focus on items with sentimental value and gained elegance over time. When you surround yourself only with cherished belongings, you can transform your home into a place filled with precious memories.

Surprisingly, despite its abundance, one thing that is often missing a person's space is color. In addition to colorful wall designs and curtains, flowers are the best way to add color. Having flowers around brightens your energy and makes your surroundings look more peaceful.

Tidying should enhance your life, not deprive you of all possessions.

When you finish tidying, your home may initially seem sparse, but the changes will be evident a few months later. After all, living a joyful, fulfilling life begins with finding and preserving the things you love and displaying them proudly in your homes.

Stowing items away temporarily to discard them later is not ideal. Doing so allows you to cling to things that do not spark joy. The choice is binary: keep or remove; if you decide to keep it, you must properly maintain it.

chapters.title: Categorize, organize, repeat
chapters.text:
When tidying seems to be going smoothly, it’s easy to get distracted. It’s also easy to be suddenly overwhelmed with anxiety, especially when you have reached a point where you have a lot to discard. You can reduce the volume of things you own yet remain undecided about where to put what’s left. You won't fully understand the extent of your possessions or the right categories for them until you've sorted everything. Consequently, consider all storage as provisional until your tidying is complete.

An important point during this process is ensuring everything stays in one category. For example, stationery goods or medical supplies should be temporarily stored in a box-like container so you can see what you have decided to keep. This approach helps you clearly see the remaining quantity of your possessions.

You can start temporary storage with three main material categories: cloth, paper, and electric equipment. These categories are easy to identify because they are the most numerous and often scattered around the house. Other items can be subdivided and placed where they’ll most likely fit in.

Store things where they belong to make it easy to find them later.

There are four principles when storing items in the previously designated categories:

• Fold it
• Stand it upright
• Store in a spot
• Organize your storage space into distinct square sections

Fold soft, pliable items like clothes, gloves, and plastic bags to compress air, maximizing storage capacity. Position objects that can stand upright in drawers to optimize vertical space and ease of retrieval.

chapters.title: Resolving clothing clutter
chapters.text:
Starting with clothes is crucial, as they occupy significant space and often contribute the most clutter in your home. First, gather all clothing from every corner of the house in one spot, ensuring nothing is left out.

Once you finish the first step, move to the “joy check” stage, where you'll hold each item to select those that genuinely spark joy. Some items may not evoke joy, yet you hesitate to part with them. In this case, determine why you need them. Sometimes, We may retain things not out of genuine liking but from a reluctance to let go.

Keep items that provide comfort, such as warmth or sentimental value.

Need help folding your clothes to save space? You can use the KonMari method as proposed by Marie Kondo. This method consists of three steps:

1. Fold both edges of the garment's body toward the center to form a rectangle.
2. Bend the long side of the rectangle in half.
3. Fold once or twice more, depending on the garment's length.

For long-sleeved tops, align the sleeves to form a rectangle and fold the sleeves down for a flatter appearance, saving space. Hoodies and turtlenecks can be folded by bringing the sides to the center, creating a rectangle, and then folding it upward.

Apply this folding technique to various garments, shaping them into rectangles for maximum space utilization and simplified organization.

chapters.title: Dealing with book overload
chapters.text:
Books are special, and it can be difficult to get rid of them, especially those you haven't read yet. A common justification for not discarding a book is the thought that it might be re-read. Yet, if a book doesn't bring joy now, the likelihood that it will later is slim. More often than not, you'll never re-read it.

For partially read or untouched books, it's wise to let go. Keep only those books that hold significant meaning, like religious texts and cookbooks. Streamlining your collection will enhance the quality of information you access. Discarding unneeded books opens space for storing those that matter most.

Giving away books can be as fulfilling as acquiring them.

Book arrangement revolves around grouping items according to their category. For books utilized in certain areas or for special purposes, store them where they're most frequently used, like placing cookbooks in the kitchen. It's not ideal to stack books in a pile; you should stand them up instead.

While it's easy to arrange books, organizing sheets of paper can be more tasking. It is easy to overlook just one sheet of paper, but the problem arises when more papers accumulate. Remember, keep only the essentials, like your children's drawings or personal notes.

chapters.title: Making more space with the Komono method
chapters.text:
Determining storage for miscellaneous items, or komono in Japanese, poses a significant challenge due to its various subcategories. Storing items like stationery supplies, cleaning supplies, kitchen goods, food, cosmetics, and laundry products can be confusing and exhausting.

Our things form a part of us, and when they’re gone, they leave behind them eternal memories. ~ Marie Kondo

The key to simple and efficient komono tidying is understanding your categories and following the previously outlined sorting process. While the process is largely similar to other storage methods, getting rid of komono is more challenging since it is necessary even though it does not spark joy.

When you come across these items, try praising them to the point where they'll spark joy. Reflect on how they make your life easier and appreciate their features. This practice helps you recognize their value and utility.

The most difficult category of komono is to discard things you've kept for no apparent reason. These items might include unused metal components like spare pins or watch straps, hairpins, extra buttons, old phone cases, replaced electronics parts, and assorted keychains.

Store items in categories based on their use to ensure you remember and utilize them when needed.

You must either discard all of them or keep them where they serve a purpose. Allocating a specific place for these items can restore their perceived value and utility.

chapters.title: Keeping memories without clutter
chapters.text:
When decluttering, remember three key points.

First, avoid passing sentimental items to others, especially your parents. Doing so only shifts the burden of clutter and makes tidying difficult for them. Items sent in boxes often remain untouched.

Secondly, if parting with an item feels impossible, choose to keep it. Trust your instincts, whether it's a cherished school event shirt or another sentimental piece. Don't criticize yourself for holding onto seemingly trivial items.

Lastly, ensure you utilize the items you've chosen to keep as you move forward.

Embrace the new by letting go of the past.

Getting rid of photos can be mentally exhausting. Photos represent memories, and letting go of memories is difficult. Choose the photos that spark joy from all the pictures you've collected. Let go of any duplicates or similar scenes. Two family photographs are enough as long as they show the same people in the pictures. Place your selected photos in an album you like close to where you store your books.

chapters.title: It’s more than having a clean house
chapters.text:
Keeping your entranceway as empty as possible is preferable, as clutter gives off negative energy that can wear you out mentally. If you want to decorate the entrance to your house, keep the entranceway minimal; reserve elaborate decorations for the interior. Opt for subtle floral arrangements to maintain a welcoming vibe.

If you are cleaning or tidying, don't forget the kitchen. Moisture and oil contribute to dirt, so wipe surfaces regularly. Avoid keeping anything near the stove or sink that can spill or pour over. Keep pots and pans to a bare minimum, storing them after use. Cooking utensils should be organized in one place, and foodstuffs should be stored vertically. Decorate your kitchen by adding bright colors and wall decorations that enhance the kitchen's aesthetic.

By clearing your living space, you declutter your mind.

Clearing even minor items like papers can mentally free you, promoting focus and a sense of importance for your surroundings. Tidying also influences your relationship pattern in ways you might not notice. Those who aren’t in relationships tend to accumulate many old clothes and papers, while those in an undefined relationship tend to be careless with their things. However, those in defined relationships are more aware of their surroundings. Your relationships with things heavily influence your relationship with other people.

Did you know? According to the National Association of Professional Organizers, about 54% of Americans feel overwhelmed by home clutter, with 78% unsure how to manage it.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Tidying is more than physical cleansing. It is a mental and spiritual exercise that helps you discover what you like around your home. When you have a tidy house, you’re more likely to experience peace of mind and approach situations with a clearer view.

To avoid clutter, try not to mix dirty clothes with clean clothes. Designated places for your belongings make it easier for you to have a clean space. Put your bags and luggage where the others are, take your dirty clothes to the laundry or throw them in the laundry basket, and hang your jacket or sweater where they belong.

It’s best to pursue simplicity when it comes to storage. Avoid hiding things or pushing them into small areas. Big storage compartments might make your space look nice, but more often than not, they are not ideal to use. They take up a lot of room and can be tricky to store.

If you live alone, it is easier to clean and tidy than if you have a family with you. If you have family members staying with you, you must create designated storage spaces for each individual. Doing this will make it easier for them to know where to check if they want anything of theirs.

You can spark joy in your life and your family when you commit to making your space tidy. As you focus on cleaning and decluttering, your soul will be more content.

Try this



• When you have time, start tidying your living space room by room.
• Assess your attitude toward every object before discarding it.
• When you return home, place your belongings in their designated places.
• Cultivate an appreciation for each item's contribution to your day.

Read it till the end?
